file.content:
do  -- tests for '%p' format
  -- not much to test, as C does not specify what '%p' does.
  -- ("The value of the pointer is converted to a sequence of printing
  -- characters, in an implementation-defined manner.")
  local null = "(null)"    -- nulls are formatted by Lua
  assert(string.format("%p", 4) == null)
  assert(string.format("%p", true) == null)
  assert(string.format("%p", nil) == null)
  assert(string.format("%p", {}) ~= null)
  assert(string.format("%p", print) ~= null)
  assert(string.format("%p", coroutine.running()) ~= null)
  assert(string.format("%p", io.stdin) ~= null)
  assert(string.format("%p", io.stdin) == string.format("%p", io.stdin))
  assert(string.format("%p", print) == string.format("%p", print))
  assert(string.format("%p", print) ~= string.format("%p", assert))

  assert(#string.format("%90p", {}) == 90)
  assert(#string.format("%-60p", {}) == 60)
  assert(string.format("%10p", false) == string.rep(" ", 10 - #null) .. null)
  assert(string.format("%-12p", 1.5) == null .. string.rep(" ", 12 - #null))

  do
    local t1 = {}; local t2 = {}
    assert(string.format("%p", t1) ~= string.format("%p", t2))
  end

  do     -- short strings are internalized
    local s1 = string.rep("a", 10)
    local s2 = string.rep("aa", 5)
  assert(string.format("%p", s1) == string.format("%p", s2))

953	
954	// ── Registration table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
955	
956	/// All base-library functions registered into the global table by `open`.
957	///
958	/// C: `static const luaL_Reg base_funcs[]`
959	///
960	/// PORT NOTE: The C table includes placeholder entries
961	/// `{LUA_GNAME, NULL}` and `{"_VERSION", NULL}` that `luaopen_base` fills in
962	/// separately.  Those are omitted here; `open()` sets them explicitly.
963	pub(crate) const BASE_FUNCS: &[(&[u8], LuaLibFn)] = &[
964	    (b"assert",         assert_fn),
965	    (b"collectgarbage", collectgarbage_fn),
966	    (b"dofile",         dofile_fn),
967	    (b"error",          error_fn),
968	    (b"getmetatable",   getmetatable_fn),
969	    (b"ipairs",         ipairs_fn),
970	    (b"loadfile",       loadfile_fn),
971	    (b"load",           load_fn),
972	    (b"next",           next_fn),
973	    (b"pairs",          pairs_fn),
974	    (b"pcall",          pcall_fn),
975	    (b"print",          print_fn),
976	    (b"warn",           warn_fn),
977	    (b"rawequal",       rawequal_fn),
978	    (b"rawlen",         rawlen_fn),
979	    (b"rawget",         rawget_fn),
980	    (b"rawset",         rawset_fn),
981	    (b"select",         select_fn),
982	    (b"setmetatable",   setmetatable_fn),
983	    (b"tonumber",       tonumber_fn),
984	    (b"tostring",       tostring_fn),
985	    (b"type",           type_fn),
986	    (b"xpcall",         xpcall_fn),
987	];
988	
989	// ── Module opener ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
990	
991	/// Open the base library: register all base functions into the global table,
992	/// then set `_G` (a self-reference) and `_VERSION`.
993	///
994	/// C: `LUAMOD_API int luaopen_base(lua_State *L)`
995	pub fn open(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
996	    // C: lua_pushglobaltable(L);
997	    state.push_globals()?;
998	    // C: luaL_setfuncs(L, base_funcs, 0);
999	    state.set_funcs(BASE_FUNCS, 0)?;
1000	    // C: lua_pushvalue(L, -1); lua_setfield(L, -2, LUA_GNAME);
1001	    state.push_copy(-1)?;
1002	    state.set_field(-2, LUA_GNAME)?;
1003	    // C: lua_pushliteral(L, LUA_VERSION); lua_setfield(L, -2, "_VERSION");
1004	    state.push_string(LUA_VERSION_STR)?;
1005	    state.set_field(-2, b"_VERSION")?;
1006	    Ok(1)
1007	}
1008	
1009	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then

delta.text:
green (only pre-existing warnings, no errors).

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_l
ib.rs:1691` — the `%p` format handler used `to_pointer(...).unwrap_or(0)` and printed `"0x0"` wh
enever the argument wasn't a pointer-collectable value. C-Lua instead writes the
`), and also honors the spec's width/`-` flag. The first failing assertion was `assert(string.format("%p
Replaced the `b'p'` arm so that when `to_pointer` returns `None` it emits `"(null)"`; when it returns `Some(p)` it emits `"
0x{p:x}"`. The handler now also parses the spec between `%` and `p` for the `-` flag and the width dig

its, and space-pads accordingly (matching tests at strings.lua:176-179).

**State

content.text:
Build is green (only pre-existing warnings, no errors).

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1691` — the `%p` format handler used `to_pointer(...).unwrap_or(0)` and printed `"0x0"` whenever the argument wasn't a pointer-collectable value. C-Lua instead writes the literal `"(null)"` (per `lstrlib.c:1336-1339`), and also honors the spec's width/`-` flag. The first failing assertion was `assert(string.format("%p", 4) == "(null)")` at strings.lua:165 (combined-file line 174).

**Fix:** Replaced the `b'p'` arm so that when `to_pointer` returns `None` it emits `"(null)"`; when it returns `Some(p)` it emits `"0x{p:x}"`. The handler now also parses the spec between `%` and `p` for the `-` flag and the width digits, and space-pads accordingly (matching tests at strings.lua:176-179).

**State after fix:** Re-tested only the build (per instructions). The `%p` formatting now matches C-Lua for non-pointer arguments and applies width/left-align — the mega-loop should advance past the line-165 assert. I did not re-run the full strings.lua test, since the task instructs not to.

C-Lua instead writes the literal `"(null)"` (per `lstrlib.c:1336-1339`), and also honors the spec's width/`-` flag. The first failing assertion was `assert(string.format("%p", 4) == "(null)")` at strings.lua:165 (combined-file line 174).

**Fix:** Replaced the `b'p'` arm so that when `to_pointer` returns `None` it emits `"(null)"`; when it returns `Some(p)` it emits `"0x{p:x}"`. The handler now also parses the spec between `%` and `p` for the `-` flag and the width digits, and space-pads accordingly (matching tests at strings.lua:176-179).
